title: Advanced Criminal Law

jokes.text: probably the same as teacher life, but less tragic, because i get to leave.
jokes.setup: Dean asks Jeff how student life is
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical comparison highlighting his temporary status
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor that establishes Jeff's detached, superior attitude. Well-structured with good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: How is student life treating you, Jeff?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's fine. You know, I'm just here temporarily until I get my degree and can move on with my life. I'm not really invested in the whole college experience thing.

jokes.text: very dry. very witty. not a great friend.
jokes.setup: Jeff's sarcastic response about student life
jokes.punchline: Dean's literal assessment of Jeff's personality
jokes.explanation: Dean's oblivious, matter-of-fact delivery makes this land. Good economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: What can I say? I'm really embracing the whole student life thing.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Yes, I've noticed. You've really thrown yourself into it. Mostly into yourself, but into it nonetheless.

jokes.text: the blonde with the pouty, strident cate blanchett sexuality and the ridiculous name?
jokes.setup: Dean asking about a girl in Jeff's class
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and pretentious description ending with 'ridiculous name'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Dean's pretentious vocabulary contrasted with shallow observation.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: So tell me about this girl in your class. What's she like?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: She's smart, funny, has this whole indie aesthetic going on. Dark hair, vintage glasses, probably listens to bands you've never heard of.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Sounds lovely! What's her name?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Britta.

jokes.text: you know, you have the savoir faire of a hyena. how is it that you and james bond come from the same island?
jokes.setup: Dean's crude approach to asking about Britta
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sophisticated insult comparing Dean to a hyena while referencing British stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Excellent sophisticated insult that shows Jeff's wit while playing on British cultural references. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're like a hyena that learned to speak English and immediately started complaining about the weather.

jokes.text: cheers. m.a.s.h. fawlty towers. game over.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to match Jeff's TV reference game
jokes.punchline: Jeff effortlessly wins by naming superior British comedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and structure. Jeff's casual dominance in this cultural exchange is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Okay Jeff, I've been studying. Name a TV show.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: The Office.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: British or American?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: American.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Ugh, amateur hour. The British version is clearly superior.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. Fawlty Towers.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I... that's... well played, Winger. Well played.

jokes.text: yeah, luis guzman is on a private jet from hollywood, because he can't wait to have that conversation with you.
jokes.setup: Character asking if Luis Guzman will visit his statue
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic response about Guzman's eagerness
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response from Jeff. Functional but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, I'm sure Luis Guzman's gonna be thrilled to come visit his own statue. He'll probably clear his whole schedule.

jokes.text: really? how does he know about me?
jokes.setup: Jeff's sarcastic comment about Luis Guzman
jokes.punchline: Troy's naive, literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - his innocent literalness creates the comedy. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Luis Guzman could be anyone's dad.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Really? I always thought he looked like Luis Guzman.

jokes.text: because he called me on my cell phone.
jokes.setup: Troy asking how Luis Guzman knows about him
jokes.punchline: Jeff continuing the absurd premise
jokes.explanation: Jeff escalating the joke setup for Troy's benefit. Serviceable but not standout.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: He's Luis Guzman. He knows about everyone. He probably knows about you right now. He's watching. He's always watching.

jokes.text: i'm president obama's nephew.
jokes.setup: Jeff pretending Luis Guzman called him
jokes.punchline: Troy's equally absurd counter-claim
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character moment. Troy's specific choice of Obama is both random and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Luis Guzmán just called me.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, I'm dating Beyoncé.

jokes.text: starting with me inventing the ferrari.
jokes.setup: Troy claiming to be Obama's nephew
jokes.punchline: Even more absurd follow-up claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation. The specificity of 'Ferrari' makes it funnier than a generic car invention claim.

jokes.text: i guess the cell phone number you put on the study group's contact sheet was fake, which i just learned in the awkward conclusion of a month-long text message affair with a dude from boulder.
jokes.setup: Previous fake phone number setup
jokes.punchline: Annie's overly detailed explanation of the awkward consequences
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Annie - her tendency to overshare awkward details. Specific enough to be funny.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, I may have given him a fake number, but I wrote down the wrong fake number, so now some innocent woman in Denver is getting calls from a very confused man who thinks he met me at the Greendale coffee cart, and she's started calling back asking who I am, so now I have to block her, but she keeps using different numbers, and I'm pretty sure she's hired a private investigator because yesterday someone showed up at my dorm asking about my whereabouts on the night of the Greendale mixer, and I almost got arrested for identity fraud, which technically isn't fraud because I didn't steal her identity, I just... accidentally ruined her life.

jokes.text: i'll cleanse my palate while kevin rethinks his marriage.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining the aftermath of the fake number
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of the serious consequences for the Boulder guy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and casual. Annie's matter-of-fact tone about Kevin's marital crisis is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: So I gave him a fake number, right? And apparently he showed up at the police station asking for me by name. They had to call my parents. My mom cried for like an hour.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: The worst part? He got arrested for something completely unrelated while he was there.

jokes.text: uh...pass.
jokes.setup: Britta asking for his number with friendship-only conditions
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate rejection of friendship-only terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency. Jeff's blunt honesty about his intentions is both funny and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, I appreciate the offer, but I'm going to have to pass on the friendship-only thing. I didn't go to law school to have terms and conditions.

jokes.text: my name's alex, dude.
jokes.setup: Chang calling him Star-Burns
jokes.punchline: His frustrated correction that no one acknowledges
jokes.explanation: Classic running gag setup. The futility of his correction is the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Star-Burns.

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: It's Alex. My name is Alex.

jokes.text: Otherwise known as a crib sheet. Who did it, huh? Marianne? [comical voice] grandpa? Jackee?
jokes.setup: Chang revealing someone cheated
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate way of calling out students with stereotypical names
jokes.explanation: Shows Chang's problematic teaching style and casual racism

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You! With the Asian name!

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: And you! With the Hispanic name!

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: This is a safe space, people. A safe space where I can just call you out however I want.

jokes.text: there's one asian stereotype that does apply to me. whoever did this insulted my honor.
jokes.setup: Chang talking about the cheating incident
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of playing into Asian stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor that acknowledges the stereotype while having Chang lean into it anyway. Self-aware character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I know what you're thinking. 'Chang cheated? How surprising. An Asian guy who's good at math.'

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Well, joke's on you. I'm not even that good at math. I'm just playing into your stereotypes because it's easier.

jokes.text: or mr. miyagi here will wax off everyone's score and the whole class gets a cero!
jokes.setup: Chang threatening the class about cheating
jokes.punchline: 'Wax off' Karate Kid reference combined with Spanish 'cero'
jokes.explanation: Chang doubling down on Asian references while mixing in Spanish. Character-appropriate but not groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You think you can cheat in my class? I will find you. And when I do, it will be like the Karate Kid. Wax on, wax off, cero.

jokes.text: the only difference between señor chang and stalin is that i know who señor chang is.
jokes.setup: Chang's authoritarian behavior in class
jokes.punchline: Jeff comparing him to Stalin with the twist that Chang is more relevant to their lives
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and unexpected twist. The logic is both absurd and sound - Chang is more immediately threatening than historical Stalin.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Chang, you're like Stalin. Except Stalin's been dead for 60 years, so he's actually less of a dictator in our daily lives.

jokes.text: did you hear him call me jackee like i'm some black female caricature?
jokes.setup: Chang's roll call behavior
jokes.punchline: Shirley's specific and justified offense at the reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Shirley's awareness and righteous anger. Specific cultural reference lands well.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I find that offensive! My name is Shirley, not Sheryl. There's a big difference!

jokes.text: if i'm gonna cheat, i'm not gonna write information from a book onto a piece of paper. that's practically learning, for god's sakes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of who might have cheated
jokes.punchline: Jeff's twisted logic about what constitutes real cheating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff logic - he's so lazy that traditional cheating seems like too much work. Perfect character integration and twisted reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, there's a difference between cheating and cheating. If you're with someone and you're physically intimate with another person, that's cheating. But if you're just emotionally connected to someone else while you're in a relationship, that's not cheating, that's just being human.

jokes.text: i may be naive, but i'm not stupid. well, i may be stupid, but i'm not trying to look like i'm not. well, i may be a genius, but i'm not a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Chain reaction of defensive statements
jokes.punchline: Pierce's completely unrelated and inappropriate contribution
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure that builds to Pierce's completely inappropriate and random comment. Perfect character moments for all three.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I was just trying to help!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Well, nobody asked you to!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I was being considerate!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You were being condescending!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know what? Back in my day, we didn't have 'considerate.' We had common sense. And a lot more black people in prison.

jokes.text: smooth move, metamucil.
jokes.setup: Annie complaining about not using Pierce for the school song
jokes.punchline: Pierce's attempt at a cutting insult using a laxative brand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - trying to be cool and cutting but using an old-person reference that makes him sound even more out of touch.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why didn't you use Pierce for the school song?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh, that's real nice. Real nice. You know what? You're being a real Metamucil.

jokes.text: troy invited rap music, and he's related to danny glover and president obama.
jokes.setup: Previous Troy lies about his connections
jokes.punchline: Abed deadpan recounting Troy's absurd claims as fact
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Abed's literal nature. 'Invited rap music' is a nice addition to Troy's mythology.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy said he once arm-wrestled a NASA astronaut and won. He also mentioned he invented a dance move that was almost adopted by the military for recruitment purposes. Oh, and he's pretty sure he's distantly related to Levar Burton.

jokes.text: abed, have you been racist this whole time while i'm telling everybody at church what a sweet little caramel angel you are?
jokes.setup: Abed seemingly believing Troy's lies
jokes.punchline: Shirley's horrified realization combined with her own questionable phrasing
jokes.explanation: Great Shirley moment - her genuine concern mixed with her own unconsciously patronizing language. 'Caramel angel' is perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Wait, you're telling me Troy lied to us?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, he's been lying this whole time.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh my God! That's terrible! I can't believe Troy would do that to us! We trusted him! We believed in him! And now... now I feel like such a fool!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm gonna go home and... I'm gonna beat my children.

jokes.text: yes. just kidding. no. like that?
jokes.setup: Troy asking if Abed has been messed with before
jokes.punchline: Abed's analytical attempt to understand the concept of joking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Abed moment showing his meta-analytical approach to basic social interactions. Perfect character writing.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Have you been messed with before?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm trying to understand the concept of joking. You're asking if I've experienced mockery, but phrasing it as a physical violation to create cognitive dissonance, which is the basis of humor. The answer is yes, but I'm not sure if acknowledging that you're trying to make me uncomfortable defeats the purpose.

jokes.text: this isn't a table. ha ha! that's funny.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to understand joking
jokes.punchline: His mechanical attempt at humor followed by self-analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of Abed's analytical approach to humor. His clinical delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to try something. Why did the chicken cross the road? To get to the other side. That's funny because... chickens are birds, and the road is a barrier, so the juxtaposition of a bird crossing a human construct is inherently absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I don't think I'm doing this right.

jokes.text: that's right. we are mature! too mature to sit in a class with a cheating, lying poop-face!
jokes.setup: Chang encouraging the class to turn on Britta
jokes.punchline: Students claiming maturity while using juvenile insult 'poop-face'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure - claiming maturity while being completely immature. The specificity of 'poop-face' makes it land.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Come on, people. Turn on Britta. She's the worst.

dialogue.speaker: Student 1
dialogue.line: Yeah, Britta's a poop-face!

dialogue.speaker: Student 2
dialogue.line: Total poop-face!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Seriously? We're better than this.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: No, no. Let it out. She's a poop-face.

jokes.text: oh, wait, i gave a quarter to a dirty bum today.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining how power dynamics have shifted with Britta
jokes.punchline: Comparing his charitable act to his current relationship with Britta
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever Jeff line showing his casual cruelty. The comparison is unexpected and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I used to be the one doing people favors. Now I'm the one owing favors. It's like I've become the charity case in our relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh really? Name one time recently where you did something nice for me without expecting something in return.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I... well, I'm always here for you emotionally.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not a favor, Britta. That's just you practicing your amateur psychology on me.

jokes.text: much like a university would!
jokes.setup: Dean explaining disciplinary procedures
jokes.punchline: His desperate attempt to sound prestigious
jokes.explanation: Standard Dean insecurity about Greendale's status. Good character consistency but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The disciplinary committee will convene to discuss the alleged infraction. We take these matters very seriously at Greendale Community College — a prestigious institution of higher learning.

jokes.text: using its $6,000 judge's table!
jokes.setup: Dean describing the tribunal location
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and probably inflated cost of pool furniture
jokes.explanation: Good Dean moment - his pride in arbitrary expensive purchases. The specificity of the amount is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The tribunal will be held in the east wing study lounge. We had to rent it out, of course. Do you know how much pool furniture costs? Twelve thousand dollars.

jokes.text: relative to this place, i'm alan dershowitz.
jokes.setup: Jeff offering to represent Britta
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to famous lawyer by Greendale's low standards
jokes.explanation: Smart Jeff line that acknowledges both his legal background and Greendale's low standards. Good cultural reference.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'll represent you. By Greendale standards, I'm basically Atticus Finch.

jokes.text: i can neither confirm nor deny that. see how good i am?
jokes.setup: Britta calling out Jeff's motives
jokes.punchline: Jeff using lawyer speak then immediately bragging about it
jokes.explanation: Great Jeff moment - his ego can't let a clever response go unnoticed. Meta-commentary on his own performance.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You're only doing this because you want to look good.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not sure I'm following your logic, and I feel like that's a personal attack. However, if we're to examine the facts, yes, I look tremendous.

jokes.text: hey, troy. did you hear? all dogs are blue now. every single dog in the world is blue.
jokes.setup: Abed attempting to mess with Troy
jokes.punchline: His completely absurd and obviously false claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed approach to lying - completely absurd and unbelievable because he doesn't understand subtlety.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, I need to tell you something. I'm actually a sophisticated android sent from the future to monitor your behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What? No you're not.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yes I am. Also, I've been replacing your deodorant with a government tracking device, and your shoes are sentient.

jokes.text: first of all, i cannot be got, because i am not gullible like you. secondly, you are not good at this, because you are not believable in your face, okay? your face? it's bad.
jokes.setup: Abed's terrible attempt at messing with Troy
jokes.punchline: Troy's detailed breakdown of why Abed failed, ending with brutal face criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy response with escalating criticism ending in the devastating 'Your face? It's bad.' Perfect structure and delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Abed, that was the worst prank attempt I've ever seen. First of all, you didn't even commit to the bit—you broke character halfway through. Second, your timing was off by like three seconds, which completely killed the misdirection. Third, and I hate to say this, but your face during the setup was way too obvious. Like, your eyebrows were doing that thing where they go up and you look like you're about to sneeze. It's very hard to take seriously.

jokes.text: well, it sounds like there are two biases, one on each side, and i go both ways. oh, let's strike that.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining his impartiality
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo followed by embarrassed correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean moment - accidental innuendo that he immediately regrets. Sets up his character's sexual confusion nicely.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I pride myself on my impartiality. I don't play favorites with any of my students.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I treat them all equally, whether they're coming or going.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I mean—arriving or departing. From Greendale.

jokes.text: it was a tuesday, uh, crack of dawn, 5:58 a.m. i got up, i took my morning deuce, i made a breakfast consisting of..
jokes.setup: Chang giving testimony about finding the cheat sheet
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed and inappropriate personal information
jokes.explanation: Standard Chang inappropriate oversharing. Character-consistent but not particularly creative or surprising.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I was in the bathroom, examining my skin condition under the fluorescent lighting, which really brings out the psoriasis, when I noticed the cheat sheet taped to the inside of the toilet tank lid. I was there for quite some time. I have IBS. Also, I've been seeing someone, but it's not serious, though she did say some hurtful things about my body that I'm still processing...

jokes.text: you can tell the dean i'll have a song that'll make the devil poop god's pants.
jokes.setup: Pierce boasting about his songwriting progress
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical and inappropriate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Pierce line. The imagery is completely absurd and inappropriate but delivered with total confidence. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I've been working on this song, and let me tell you, the lyrics are really coming together. I'm talking verses, chorus, the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's like... it's like taking a box of frozen fish sticks and teaching them to tap dance while riding a motorcycle through a car wash made of cheese.

jokes.text: your sheet music looks like a chinese takeout menu.
jokes.setup: Annie looking at Pierce's songwriting attempts
jokes.punchline: Comparison of his music notation to food menu
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke that we can infer from the dialogue. Sets up Pierce's next line perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, this doesn't even look like sheet music. It looks like a Chinese takeout menu.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, maybe I'm composing in a language you don't understand.

jokes.text: moo shoo pork, squab with lettuce. you see? i mean, it just comes out.
jokes.setup: Annie's Chinese menu comment
jokes.punchline: Pierce turning the insult into proof of his musical genius
jokes.explanation: Excellent Pierce logic - turning criticism into validation of his artistic process. The specific food items make it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Your Chinese menu comment was racist.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Racist? That's not racist, that's observational comedy. I'm a musical genius!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, you can't just say offensive things and call it comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: The fact that you're upset proves I'm doing something right. That's what geniuses do—they challenge people.

jokes.text: you are inside a throbbing, cosmic womb of creativity, and when this baby starts kicking, i cannot be responsible for your sanity.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining why Annie should leave
jokes.punchline: Completely over-the-top artistic pretention with disturbing imagery
jokes.explanation: Fantastic Pierce pretension with disturbing biological metaphors. The extended pregnancy imagery is both gross and hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Annie, you need to leave this study group. I'm going to explain why in the form of an interpretive dance piece that questions the very nature of your existence and features me as a sentient pile of bones representing the death of your dreams.

jokes.text: if you know what i mean.
jokes.setup: Chang's testimony about Britta's behavior
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual innuendo about 'tampon time'
jokes.explanation: Weak Chang moment - relies on juvenile menstruation humor. Character-appropriate but not clever.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: During 'tampon time,' she becomes a dangerous predator.

jokes.text: objection. i don't know what he means.
jokes.setup: Chang's inappropriate tampon comment
jokes.punchline: Jeff's deadpan refusal to acknowledge the crude innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff response - using legal formality to shut down Chang's crudeness. Great economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You know what they say about tampons? They're a lot like our military strategy in the Middle East - they're supposed to absorb the mess but they just make things worse.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not going to acknowledge that.

jokes.text: shut your pompous vortex of overlapping fangs!
jokes.setup: Chang angry at Duncan's interruption
jokes.punchline: Elaborate and creative insult about British teeth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chang insult - creative, specific, and completely over-the-top. 'Vortex of overlapping fangs' is poetry.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm tired of your British interruptions, Duncan. Your teeth look like a British graveyard – all crooked, all dead, and nobody's visited in centuries!

jokes.text: hey, british dentistry is not on trial.
jokes.setup: Chang's elaborate dental insult
jokes.punchline: Duncan's defensive but self-aware response
jokes.explanation: Good Duncan comeback that acknowledges the stereotype while defending himself. Meta-awareness lands well.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Your teeth look like a broken picket fence in a trailer park.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Well, at least I have teeth. And yes, I know they're not perfect, but they're mine and they work.

jokes.text: the world wasn't the only thing that changed on september 11th.
jokes.setup: Jeff making a dramatic legal argument
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately invoking 9/11 for a cheating case
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing Jeff's complete lack of proportion and shame. The inappropriate gravitas is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Your Honor, we need to talk about what happened on September 11th.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Objection!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: My client was betrayed. Thousands of innocent people were hurt. And yes, I'm talking about when Chad cheated on his girlfriend.

jokes.text: save the table! save the table!
jokes.setup: Pool area accident during tribunal
jokes.punchline: Dean's priority is the expensive table, not people's safety
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the expensive table. Dean's priorities are perfectly wrong and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Is everyone okay? Wait, never mind. Is the table okay?

jokes.text: Abed ululating and making alien transmission sounds
jokes.setup: Abed's anthropological observation escalating
jokes.punchline: Full commitment to alien observer persona with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Abed's character quirk with full physical commitment

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm observing human mating rituals. *makes clicking sounds* The male displays his plumage while the female assesses his resource accumulation capacity. *more clicking* Fascinating. We must document this for the Zorgon Council.

jokes.text: it is on like donkey kong!
jokes.setup: Chang challenging Duncan to a fight
jokes.punchline: Using outdated slang to sound threatening
jokes.explanation: Decent Chang moment showing his inappropriate aggression and dated references. Character-consistent but not groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You want a piece of this? I'm gonna take you to Flavortown!

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: That's... not threatening at all.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, prepare to get served! And I don't mean food, homes!

jokes.text: do you mind if we have this conversation in a room with less balls?
jokes.setup: Chang challenging him in the pool area
jokes.punchline: Duncan's sophisticated way of commenting on the location
jokes.explanation: Good Duncan line - maintaining his sophistication while making a pool pun. Works on multiple levels.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: You know, I've always found the aquatic center to be a rather elegant venue for conflict resolution.

jokes.text: look how handsome my face is.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to convince Britta of his sincerity
jokes.punchline: Using his attractiveness as evidence of his honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff logic - his vanity is so complete he thinks his looks prove his character. Great economy and self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, I'm being completely honest with you. Look at me. Do I look like someone who would lie?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Jeff, you're incredibly handsome. That's literally the easiest thing to lie about.

jokes.text: gentlemen... my client is insane.
jokes.setup: Jeff promising a great defense strategy
jokes.punchline: Completely throwing Britta under the bus as his defense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and perfect structure. Jeff's complete betrayal of Britta as his defense strategy is both shocking and hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I have a great defense strategy. It's simple, really.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We pin the whole thing on Britta.

jokes.text: you two are arguing about status at a college that correspondence schools make fun of.
jokes.setup: Jeff pointing out everyone's flaws
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Greendale's academic standing
jokes.explanation: Excellent roast of Greendale. The hierarchy joke (correspondence schools looking down on them) is perfectly structured.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I'm just being honest. This school is a joke. We have a dean who dresses like he raided a costume shop, a biology teacher who's clearly making it up as he goes, and a library that's basically a front for whatever the hell else is happening in this place.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: The curriculum is a disaster, half our classes don't even have real textbooks, and I'm pretty sure our accreditation is held together by duct tape and whatever favors the dean owes to people in dark suits.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Greendale Community College isn't an institution of higher learning — it's a tax write-off with a cafeteria.

jokes.text: you are putting us at risk of electrocution.
jokes.setup: Jeff criticizing the Dean's tribunal setup
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the dangerous pool/electricity combination
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor pointing out the absurd safety hazard. Works as both comedy and plot point.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This tribunal is ridiculous. You've got a swimming pool right next to all this electrical equipment. Do you want someone to die? Because this is how you get someone to die.

jokes.text: oh, come on, leonard. if you're gonna argue with me, put on a bathing suit.
jokes.setup: Leonard objecting to Jeff's 'everyone is crazy' defense
jokes.punchline: Jeff pointing out Leonard's inappropriate pool attire
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy we can infer - Leonard presumably in inappropriate pool attire. Jeff's delivery makes this land.

dialogue.speaker: Leonard
dialogue.line: That's not a defense, that's just you being lazy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm lazy? Leonard, you're wearing a Speedo to the community pool.

dialogue.speaker: Leonard
dialogue.line: It's efficient.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's a cry for help.

jokes.text: if crazy people can't be at greendale, where are we supposed to go?
jokes.setup: Jeff's defense argument about accepting crazy people
jokes.punchline: Implication that Greendale is the bottom of the barrel for education
jokes.explanation: Strong closing argument that's both touching and insulting. Good structure building to the emotional core of the show.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I'm not saying we should accept crazy people. I'm saying that Greendale already has. We're not the bottom of the barrel for education—we're the barrel itself.

jokes.text: thinking maybe fridays, nine-ish, something like that.
jokes.setup: Duncan recommending psychological counseling for Britta
jokes.punchline: Suspiciously specific scheduling that benefits him personally
jokes.explanation: Great Duncan moment showing his opportunistic nature. The casual 'nine-ish' makes his self-interest obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Britta, I really think you should see a therapist. How about Tuesdays and Thursdays from 2 to 4 PM? Those are the only times that work for me... I mean, for your schedule.

jokes.text: it would be less creepy if you were actually an alien.
jokes.setup: Abed's elaborate alien performance
jokes.punchline: Troy's assessment that the fake version is worse than the real thing would be
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy observation about Abed's behavior. The twisted logic that reality would be less disturbing is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This fake version is worse than the real thing would be.

jokes.text: i'm no more of a songwriter than you or billy joel.
jokes.setup: Pierce confessing he's a fraud
jokes.punchline: Grouping Billy Joel with amateurs in his comparison
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce moment showing his complete lack of perspective. Casually dismissing Billy Joel as an amateur is perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm a fraud. I've never actually listened to Billy Joel.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I just nod along whenever someone mentions 'Uptown Girl' like I know what they're talking about.

jokes.text: you'll be wiping off that ketchup with our wipes, hawthorne wipes! you'll be wiping off that mustard with our wipes at the picnic!
jokes.setup: Pierce revealing his 'original' jingle
jokes.punchline: Obviously stolen melody with terrible lyrics
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Pierce's earlier boast. The terrible lyrics over a familiar melody show his complete lack of talent.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: This is my original jingle that I wrote myself.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's the Meow Mix jingle.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No it's not.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: It's literally the Meow Mix jingle.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well I wrote better lyrics for it.

jokes.text: you're not very pretty, you have no boobs, and you can't do a basket toss to save your life, but you made a commitment.
jokes.setup: Annie giving Pierce a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Her mother's brutally honest cheerleading advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Annie moment revealing her mother's harsh parenting style. The escalating cruelty followed by 'but you made a commitment' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You know what, Pierce? You can do this. You're stronger than you think.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: My mother always said, 'Annie, you're not the prettiest, you're not the smartest, and honestly, you're kind of a mess. But you're persistent, and that's something.'

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: So that's what I'm telling you. You're going to be fine.

jokes.text: so pick up your pompoms, pierce, stuff your bra, and get ready for the team bus to forget you at a taco bell, because life is tough.
jokes.setup: Annie continuing her mother's advice
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and depressing cheerleading experiences
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation with incredibly specific depressing details. 'Team bus to forget you at a Taco Bell' is inspired.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: My mother always said, 'Annie, be a cheerleader.'

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: So I did. I was a cheerleader at my old school.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You know what that taught me? That the football team doesn't respect you. They don't even know your name.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: And the other cheerleaders? They're just as competitive and backstabbing as you'd think.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Plus, my uniform was a size too small because apparently that's 'motivating' for the crowd.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: And one time, I got dropped during a pyramid. DROPPED. During homecoming.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: In front of my parents. Who were eating nachos and didn't even notice because they were arguing about my dad's fantasy football league.

jokes.text: does he know he stole it? i don't think so. let him enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Pierce performing his 'original' song
jokes.punchline: Recognition that Pierce is obliviously plagiarizing but deciding to let it slide
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing protective affection for Pierce despite his obvious fraud. Sweet and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's... that's a beautiful song, Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Yes, quite moving. Very original.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Completely original. No one has ever written anything like that before.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Not a single person in the history of music.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We should probably just let him think that.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Agreed. For his own good.

jokes.text: can we get sued? - not sure.
jokes.setup: Recognition that Pierce plagiarized a famous song
jokes.punchline: Casual uncertainty about legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Good capper showing the group's acceptance of potential legal trouble for Pierce's sake

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce plagiarized that song! It's identical to the original!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So... what happens now? Does he get sued or something?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I mean, probably? That's usually how plagiarism works.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Probably. Great. Good to know.

tmdb_overview: As Pierce volunteers to compose a new school song, a cheating episode in Spanish class leaves Jeff defending Britta before a disciplinary tribunal.
insight: Early ensemble showcase delivers 63 jokes in 41 minutes via character-driven escalations.
